title: system: add new subcommand "migrate"
body:
it is useful to migrate existing containers to a new version of
podman.  Currently, it is needed to migrate rootless containers that
were created with podman <= 1.2 to a newer version which requires all
containers to be running in the same user namespace.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2935

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:

> Need to update command.md with an entry for this command.

do I need to add it?  It is a subcommand of system



>  Any chance to add a test? Seems hard to pull off, but thought I'd ask.

we would need to have an old version of podman preinstalled

I'll let @mheon make the call on the "should it be on commands.md" or not.  I think yes.  Currently we have separate listings for "podman container *" and "podman volume *", but we don't do the same for "podman system *". 

The test sounds like a headache, I'd not worry about it then.  Sounds like you were considering the same roadblock that I was.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2917) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@giuseppe @TomSweeneyRedHat If `system renumber` is in the commands page, we add this; if it's not, we don't. I think we ought to match what the existing similar commands do.
Is this something we should run in the postinstall?  How are we going to tell individual users that we need to run this.  Can we do a check in the podman command?
We want this only for rootless, I think
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2948) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
rebased and addressed all the comments
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2970) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2933) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
commit message/PR summary says `podman <= 1.12` -- probably meant `0.12` ?
I think that should be 1.1.2?
Possibly 1.2

> Is this something we should run in the postinstall? How are
> we going to tell individual users that we need to run this. Can
> we do a check in the podman command?

RPM scriptlets are run server-side on Silverblue, and even on the legacy Workstation there's the problem of migrating rootless containers. However, we can assume that updates are applied offline (ie., the `podman` binary won't get swapped out on a running system). Silverblue guarantees it, and is what's encouraged on legacy Workstation.

I was wondering if I could call `podman system migrate` before `podman start`? Do I need to be careful about calling it only once after the update? Or can I hard code a call in there, and rely on subsequent calls turning into NOPs?
We want to make sure that no containers are running when it does run, but it seems like it ought to be safe to run more than once given that remains true.
Needs a rebase to get the SELinux fixes.
tests are happy!

I'll hit my comments once this merges in a separate PR, let's get this landed. 
LGTM
